title: RFC: {{title}}
p: TODO: Summarize why this feature exists and what product outcome it should create. Cover what is being built, why, and why now.
Source: {{source}}

TODO: Describe how the relevant parts of the system work TODAY — before any changes from this RFC. Lead with the end-to-end user/data flow across services, then summarize gaps and constraints. Keep file paths out of the main prose except for 1-2 critical anchors.
TODO: Narrative of the existing flow from entry point to downstream service/data store, including async callbacks/events where relevant.

ul.li: TODO: Gap or limitation that matters for the proposed design.

td.p: TODO: concise fact, not full implementation notes.

p: TODO: Describe what the system looks like AFTER this RFC is fully implemented. Focus on the delta from Current State. Cover what is new, what has changed, and what has been removed or replaced.
callout: Do not finalize or push this RFC while any decision below is still inferred by the agent. Discuss the choices with the requester and either record the confirmed direction or keep it as an explicit open question.

td.p: TODO: Confirm whether to reuse/piggyback an existing API, extend an existing API, or introduce a new API. Do not assume new API creation.
TODO: Confirm which service owns each state change and which service must not be called directly.
TODO: Confirm whether linking or state update is synchronous, asynchronous, callback-driven, event-driven, or manually reconciled.
TODO: Confirm whether cache is needed, cache key/value shape, TTL, invalidation trigger, and fallback behavior.
TODO: Confirm flag/whitelist owner, targeting rule, default state, rollout phases, eligibility checks, and kill-switch behavior.
TODO: Confirm whether existing users need backfill, lazy migration, or no migration.
TODO: Confirm timeout, retry, idempotency, duplicate callback, and partial-success handling.

p: TODO: Brief summary of items covered as part of the solution.
TODO: Brief summary of items not covered as part of the solution.

TODO: Describe the recommended implementation approach as an overall system design: ownership, request/data flow, state transitions, sync/async boundaries, failure handling, and assumptions. Keep code references out of the main rationale unless essential; detailed repository evidence belongs in Current State / Evidence or the per-story Technical Approach.
As [actor/role], I want [goal or capability], so that [business benefit].
callout: A single approach is the default. Only add an "Approach #2" block when there is a genuine alternative solution to weigh; otherwise delete this note and keep one approach.
p: TODO: Describe how this story is implemented, citing concrete file:line references from the affected repositories.
whiteboard: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant Client participant BFF participant Service Client->>BFF: TODO: request BFF->>Service: TODO: downstream call Service-->>BFF: TODO: response BFF-->>Client: TODO: response
p: TODO: For every new or changed API, include owner/caller, method/path, auth boundary, idempotency behavior, timeout/retry expectations, compatibility notes, and request/success/error schemas. Put request and response examples in code blocks, not tables.
Owner / Caller: TODO: service owner / caller.
Method / Path: TODO: GET|POST /path.
Auth Boundary: TODO: authentication and authorization boundary.
Idempotency: TODO: read-only, idempotency key, duplicate callback behavior, or retry-safe state transition.
Timeout / Retry: TODO: caller timeout, retry policy, downstream fallback.
Compatibility: TODO: backwards compatibility and client rollout notes.
Error Semantics: TODO: status codes and business meanings, for example 400 validation error, 401/403 auth failure, 404 missing resource, 409 invalid state, 5xx downstream failure.
TODO: List prerequisites/dependencies on any team/component before building/rollout.

td.p: Non-Functional Requirements — latency SLA, throughput, error rate targets
Security — authentication, authorisation, data access control
Data Privacy / PII — new PII fields, data masking, retention policy
Risk & Fraud — new attack surface, fraud rule changes, risk signal impact
Customer Support (CS) Escalation — new error codes, CS tooling, escalation playbook
Backward Compatibility — breaking API changes, client migration plan
Feature Flag / Kill Switch — mechanism to enable/disable safely without redeploy
Observability — new metrics, dashboards, log events, alerting rules
Rate Limiting & Throttling — abuse prevention, quota impact on new endpoints
Third-party / External Dependencies — vendor SLA, fallback if external system is down
Testing Strategy — unit, integration, E2E, regression, load testing coverage

p: TODO: Document how this change will be deployed to production safely. Cover feature flag name, rollout phases, targeting criteria, and monitoring gate per phase.
TODO: Document how to safely reverse this change. Cover immediate action, step-by-step, user-facing implications, data safety, and who to notify.
TODO: Jot down the conclusion of the RFC review meeting discussion.
